note: Three tiers. easy/recipe: response-graded on the per-popup config object, the trigger-method and layout/type enums. medium/introspection: setup writes a known simple_popup_blocks.popup_<uid> config object live, agent reads it back with drush, cleanup deletes it. hard/execution: reset deletes any popup with the target selector so verify FAILS on empty, agent CREATES a simple_popup_blocks.popup_ config object, verify reads it live (exit 0 = pass).

evals.prompt: In the Simple Popup Blocks module, where is each popup stored? Name the config object and describe how a Drupal block is turned into a popup.
evals.expected_output: Each popup is a standalone simple config object named simple_popup_blocks.popup_<uid> (schema pattern simple_popup_blocks.*). You point it at existing markup by CSS selector: type 0 targets a Drupal block (identifier = block config id, rendered as block-<id>), type 1 targets a custom CSS id/class. The module ships no popup content or styling of its own.

evals.prompt: What trigger methods can a Simple Popup Blocks popup use, and which config key selects them? Give the values.
evals.expected_output: The trigger_method key: 0 = Automatic (shown after `delay` seconds), 1 = Manual on click of `trigger_selector` (which must start with . or #), 2 = Before browser/tab close (exit intent).

evals.prompt: In Simple Popup Blocks, which config key sets where the popup appears on screen and what value gives a centered popup? Also, what is the difference between type 0 and type 1?
evals.expected_output: The `layout` key (0-9) sets position: 0 top-left, 1 top-right, 2 bottom-left, 3 bottom-right, 4 center, 5 top-center, 6 top-bar, 7 bottom-bar, 8 left-bar, 9 right-bar — so 4 is centered. `type` 0 targets a Drupal block, `type` 1 targets a custom CSS id/class (with css_selector 0=class/./, 1=id/#).

evals.prompt: This Drupal 11 site has a Simple Popup Blocks popup configured. Inspect the live configuration and report: which element it targets (the identifier), where it appears on screen (its layout), and how it is triggered.
evals.expected_output: The popup simple_popup_blocks.popup_known_promo targets the custom CSS id promo-banner (type 1, css_selector 1), uses layout 6 (top bar), and is triggered manually on click (trigger_method 1) of trigger_selector #open-promo. Written by the setup script.

evals.prompt: A Simple Popup Blocks popup is already configured on this site. Inspect its live configuration and report how often it is shown to a visitor (its display frequency).
evals.expected_output: The popup simple_popup_blocks.popup_known_news uses time frequency instead of visit count (use_time_frequency = true) with time_frequency = 86400, i.e. shown at most once daily. Written by the setup script.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site, use Simple Popup Blocks to create an enabled popup that targets the custom CSS id #newsletter-signup, appears centered on screen, and triggers automatically on page load. Make it stick on the live site.
evals.expected_output: A simple_popup_blocks.popup_<uid> config object exists with identifier=newsletter-signup, type=1, css_selector=1 (id), layout=4 (center), trigger_method=0 (automatic), status=1. Verified against live config.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site, use Simple Popup Blocks to create an enabled popup that targets the custom CSS class .exit-offer, shows in the bottom-right corner, and is triggered just before the visitor closes the browser or tab (exit intent). Apply it to the live site.
evals.expected_output: A simple_popup_blocks.popup_<uid> config object exists with identifier=exit-offer, type=1, css_selector=0 (class), layout=3 (bottom-right), trigger_method=2 (before browser/tab close), status=1. Verified against live config.
